tactic:
-- After hcomp: A.card + B.card = n, need A.card < B.card.
        -- Suffices: A.card ≤ n/2, then omega finishes.
        -- A.card = number of voters whose peak < median
        -- In the sorted list, median is at position n/2, so at most n/2 elements are < median.
        -- We formalize: countP (< median) l ≤ n/2 using sortedness.
        have h_le : (Finset.filter (fun i => peaks i < median_peak peaks) Finset.univ).card ≤
            Fintype.card ι / 2 := by
          let l := sorted_peaks_list peaks
          have hl : l.length = Fintype.card ι := by
            unfold l sorted_peaks_list
            simp [List.length_mergeSort, List.length_map, Finset.length_toList]
          have hl_sorted : l.Pairwise (· ≤ ·) := List.pairwise_mergeSort _ _
          have hl_perm : l ≈ Finset.univ.toList.map peaks := List.mergeSort_perm _ _
          classical
          -- Transfer Finset.card to List.countP via perm
          have h1 : (Finset.filter (fun i => peaks i < median_peak peaks) Finset.univ).card =
              (Finset.univ.toList.countP fun i => peaks i < median_peak peaks) := by
            simp [Finset.card_univ, Finset.length_toList]
          have h2 : (Finset.univ.toList.countP fun i => peaks i < median_peak peaks) =
              (Finset.univ.toList.map peaks).countP (fun x => x < median_peak peaks) := by
            simp [List.countP_map]
          have h3 : (Finset.univ.toList.map peaks).countP (fun x => x < median_peak peaks) =
              l.countP (fun x => x < median_peak peaks) := by
            exact hl_perm.countP_eq
          rw [h1, h2, h3]
          -- Now need: countP (< median) l ≤ l.length / 2
          -- median = l.get ⟨n/2, by omega⟩
          have hn : l.length / 2 < l.length := by omega
          have hl_med : median_peak peaks = l.get ⟨l.length / 2, hn⟩ := by
            unfold median_peak sorted_peaks_list l
            simp [List.getD_eq_getElem hn]
          rw [hl_med]
          -- In a sorted list, countP (< l[k]) ≤ k
          sorry
tactic_norm: -- After hcomp: A.card + B.card = n, need A.card < B.card. -- Suffices: A.card ≤ n/2, then omega finishes. -- A.card = number of voters whose peak < median -- In the sorted list, median is at position n/2, so at most n/2 elements are < median. -- We formalize: countP (< median) l ≤ n/2 using sortedness. have h_le : (Finset.filter (fun i => peaks i < median_peak peaks) Finset.univ).card ≤ Fintype.card ι / 2 := by let l := sorted_peaks_list peaks have hl : l.length = Fintype.card ι := by unfold l sorted_peaks_list simp [List.length_mergeSort, List.length_map, Finset.length_toList] have hl_sorted : l.Pairwise (· ≤ ·) := List.pairwise_mergeSort _ _ have hl_perm : l ≈ Finset.univ.toList.map peaks := List.mergeSort_perm _ _ classical -- Transfer Finset.card to List.countP via perm have h1 : (Finset.filter (fun i => peaks i < median_peak peaks) Finset.univ).card = (Finset.univ.toList.countP fun i => peaks i < median_peak peaks) := by simp [Finset.card_univ, Finset.length_toList] have h2 : (Finset.univ.toList.countP fun i => peaks i < median_peak peaks) = (Finset.univ.toList.map peaks).countP (fun x => x < median_peak peaks) := by simp [List.countP_map] have h3 : (Finset.univ.toList.map peaks).countP (fun x => x < median_peak peaks) = l.countP (fun x => x < median_peak peaks) := by exact hl_perm.countP_eq rw [h1, h2, h3] -- Now need: countP (< median) l ≤ l.length / 2 -- median = l.get ⟨n/2, by omega⟩ have hn : l.length / 2 < l.length := by omega have hl_med : median_peak peaks = l.get ⟨l.length / 2, hn⟩ := by unfold median_peak sorted_peaks_list l simp [List.getD_eq_getElem hn] rw [hl_med] -- In a sorted list, countP (< l[k]) ≤ k sorry

tactic:
-- A.card ≤ n/2 via sortedness of peaks list
        have h_le : (Finset.filter (fun i => peaks i < median_peak peaks) Finset.univ).card ≤
            Fintype.card ι / 2 := by
          let l := sorted_peaks_list peaks
          have hl : l.length = Fintype.card ι := by
            unfold l sorted_peaks_list
            simp [List.length_mergeSort, List.length_map, Finset.length_toList]
          have hl_sorted : l.Pairwise (· ≤ ·) := List.pairwise_mergeSort _ _
          have hl_perm : l ≈ Finset.univ.toList.map peaks := List.mergeSort_perm _ _
          classical
          have h1 : (Finset.filter (fun i => peaks i < median_peak peaks) Finset.univ).card =
              (Finset.univ.toList.countP fun i => peaks i < median_peak peaks) := by
            simp [Finset.card_univ, Finset.length_toList]
          have h2 : (Finset.univ.toList.countP fun i => peaks i < median_peak peaks) =
              (Finset.univ.toList.map peaks).countP (fun x => x < median_peak peaks) :=
            (List.countP_map (f := peaks)).symm
          have h3 : (Finset.univ.toList.map peaks).countP (fun x => x < median_peak peaks) =
              l.countP (fun x => x < median_peak peaks) :=
            hl_perm.countP_eq
          rw [h1, h2, h3]
          have hn : l.length / 2 < l.length := by omega
          have hl_med : median_peak peaks = l.get ⟨l.length / 2, hn⟩ := by
            unfold median_peak sorted_peaks_list l
            simp [List.getD_eq_getElem hn]
          rw [hl_med]
          sorry
tactic_norm: -- A.card ≤ n/2 via sortedness of peaks list have h_le : (Finset.filter (fun i => peaks i < median_peak peaks) Finset.univ).card ≤ Fintype.card ι / 2 := by let l := sorted_peaks_list peaks have hl : l.length = Fintype.card ι := by unfold l sorted_peaks_list simp [List.length_mergeSort, List.length_map, Finset.length_toList] have hl_sorted : l.Pairwise (· ≤ ·) := List.pairwise_mergeSort _ _ have hl_perm : l ≈ Finset.univ.toList.map peaks := List.mergeSort_perm _ _ classical have h1 : (Finset.filter (fun i => peaks i < median_peak peaks) Finset.univ).card = (Finset.univ.toList.countP fun i => peaks i < median_peak peaks) := by simp [Finset.card_univ, Finset.length_toList] have h2 : (Finset.univ.toList.countP fun i => peaks i < median_peak peaks) = (Finset.univ.toList.map peaks).countP (fun x => x < median_peak peaks) := (List.countP_map (f := peaks)).symm have h3 : (Finset.univ.toList.map peaks).countP (fun x => x < median_peak peaks) = l.countP (fun x => x < median_peak peaks) := hl_perm.countP_eq rw [h1, h2, h3] have hn : l.length / 2 < l.length := by omega have hl_med : median_peak peaks = l.get ⟨l.length / 2, hn⟩ := by unfold median_peak sorted_peaks_list l simp [List.getD_eq_getElem hn] rw [hl_med] sorry
